title: Vanta Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Vanta** is a compliance-automation SaaS that continuously monitors security controls, vendor risk, personnel access, and audit evidence to maintain SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR, and PCI DSS attestations. This connector ŌĆö `VantaConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "vanta"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_client_credentials"`) ŌĆö wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from Vanta:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Frameworks | `/v1/frameworks` | List + read compliance frameworks |
| Controls | `/v1/controls` | List + read controls (optionally scoped to a framework) |
| Vendors | `/v1/vendors` | Third-party vendor inventory + risk metadata |
| Personnel | `/v1/personnel` | Employees, contractors, role assignments |
| Risks | `/v1/risks` | Risk register entries with likelihood + impact |
| Incidents | `/v1/incidents` | Security incidents with severity + status |
| Documents | `/v1/documents` | Policy / SOP documents under version control |
| Tests | `/v1/tests` | Continuous control tests + status |
| Findings | `/v1/findings` | Open findings flagged by Vanta scanners |
| Audits | `/v1/audits` | Active and historical audit engagements |

The connector normalises frameworks + controls + vendors + personnel into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{connector_id}_{kind}_{source_id}"`, tenant-scoped via `connector_id`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC ŌĆö all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::VantaHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Create an OAuth client in Vanta

1. Sign in to https://app.vanta.com.
2. Open **Settings ŌåÆ API ŌåÆ OAuth Apps**.
3. Click **Create OAuth App** and choose the **Client Credentials** flow.
4. Grant the scopes you need ŌĆö at minimum `vanta-api.all:read`; add `vanta-api.vendors:write` if you intend to create or update vendors.
5. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** ŌĆö Vanta shows the secret only once.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP ŌåÆ Manage Connectors ŌåÆ Vanta**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `client_id` (string) | OAuth client id from step 5 |
| `client_secret` (secret) | Paired secret |
| `scopes` (string, optional) | Defaults to `vanta-api.all:read vanta-api.vendors:write` |
| `base_url` (string, optional) | Override `https://api.vanta.com/v1` (rarely needed) |
| `token_url` (string, optional) | Override `https://api.vanta.com/oauth/token` |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates the credential pair, immediately mints an access token, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`. If the token endpoint returns 401, `install()` returns `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` so the operator sees the failure straight away.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which probes `GET /frameworks?pageSize=1` as a lightweight reachability test. On 2xx ŌåÆ `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 ŌåÆ `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED` (after a single token re-mint attempt). On 403 ŌåÆ `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 ŌåÆ retry with exponential backoff; if still failing ŌåÆ `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with a structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Vanta REST API uses **OAuth 2.0 client_credentials** (`AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_client_credentials"`) ŌĆö machine-to-machine, no user popup, no redirect URI.

### Credential model

- `client_id` + `client_secret` are sent form-encoded to `POST https://api.vanta.com/oauth/token` with `grant_type=client_credentials` and optional `scope`.
- The token endpoint returns `{ access_token, expires_in, token_type, scope }`.
- The access token is cached in-memory under an `asyncio.Lock` until `expires_in - 60s` (refresh leeway). After that, any request transparently re-mints.
- A single 401 response on a downstream call triggers exactly one forced re-mint + retry ŌĆö no recursion, no infinite loop.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `client_id` + `client_secret`. Mints the first access token. Returns `HEALTHY + AUTHENTICATED` on success. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` populated from the cached / minted token. |
| `health_check()` | Mints token if needed, then `GET /frameworks?pageSize=1`. |
| `ensure_token()` | Implicit ŌĆö every `_request()` checks the cache before sending. |
| token storage | In-memory only (process-local). The connector framework can layer Redis-backed storage on top via the `BaseConnector` hooks. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` ŌĆö never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `client_id` | string | yes | `self.client_id` | OAuth client id |
| `client_secret` | secret | yes | `self.client_secret` | OAuth client secret |
| `scopes` | string | no | `self.scopes` (default `vanta-api.all:read vanta-api.vendors:write`) | Space-separated scope list |
| `base_url` | string | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://api.vanta.com/v1`) | Override for sandbox or proxy |
| `token_url` | string | no | `self.token_url` (defaults to `https://api.vanta.com/oauth/token`) | OAuth token endpoint |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["client_id", "client_secret"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_VANTA_BASE_URL  = "https://api.vanta.com/v1"
_VANTA_TOKEN_URL = "https://api.vanta.com/oauth/token"
_DEFAULT_SCOPES  = "vanta-api.all:read vanta-api.vendors:write"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `VantaConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::VantaHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `client_id` + `client_secret`; mint the access token; persist install-time config.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
client_credentials grant ŌĆö `auth_code`/`state` are unused. Returns a `TokenInfo` carrying the cached / freshly-minted access token.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Probes `GET /frameworks?pageSize=1`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates Vanta frameworks, controls, vendors, personnel ŌåÆ `NormalizedDocument` ŌåÆ `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Frameworks
children.content:
### `async list_frameworks(*, page_size=50, page_cursor=None) -> Dict`
`GET /frameworks?pageSize={N}&pageCursor={C}`.

### `async get_framework(framework_id) -> Dict`
`GET /frameworks/{framework_id}`.

children.title: Controls
children.content:
### `async list_controls(*, page_size=50, page_cursor=None, framework_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /controls?pageSize={N}&pageCursor={C}&frameworkId={F}`.

### `async get_control(control_id) -> Dict`
`GET /controls/{control_id}`.

children.title: Vendors
children.content:
### `async list_vendors(*, page_size=50, page_cursor=None) -> Dict`
`GET /vendors`.

### `async get_vendor(vendor_id) -> Dict`
`GET /vendors/{vendor_id}`.

children.title: Personnel
children.content:
### `async list_personnel(*, page_size=50, page_cursor=None, includes_inactive=False) -> Dict`
`GET /personnel?pageSize={N}&pageCursor={C}&includesInactive={true|false}`.

### `async get_personnel(person_id) -> Dict`
`GET /personnel/{person_id}`.

children.title: Risks ┬Ę Incidents ┬Ę Documents
children.content:
### `async list_risks(*, page_size=50, page_cursor=None) -> Dict`
`GET /risks`.

### `async list_incidents(*, page_size=50, page_cursor=None, severity=None, status=None) -> Dict`
`GET /incidents` with optional `severity` + `status` filters.

### `async list_documents(*, page_size=50, page_cursor=None) -> Dict`
`GET /documents`.

children.title: Tests ┬Ę Findings ┬Ę Audits
children.content:
### `async list_tests(*, page_size=50, page_cursor=None, test_status=None) -> Dict`
`GET /tests` (the `test_status` arg maps to the `status` query param).

### `async list_findings(*, page_size=50, page_cursor=None, severity=None, status=None) -> Dict`
`GET /findings`.

### `async list_audits(*, page_size=50, page_cursor=None) -> Dict`
`GET /audits`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
VantaError                            # base; carries status_code + response_body
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ VantaAuthError                    # 401 / 403
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ VantaBadRequestError              # 400
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ VantaNotFoundError                # 404
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ VantaConflictError                # 409
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ VantaRateLimitError               # 429 ŌĆö retry_after_s
ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ VantaServerError                  # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
VantaNetworkError = VantaServerError  # legacy name from older code
VantaNotFound     = VantaNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 404 | Raise immediately |
| 401 | Re-mint access token once, retry the request once; if still 401 ŌåÆ raise `VantaAuthError` |
| 403 | Raise immediately (scope insufficient) |
| 429 | Honour `Retry-After` when present; otherwise exponential backoff (`0.5s ├Ś 2^attempt + jitter`, capped at 16s), max 3 retries |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, max 3 retries |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff + retry, max 3, then raise `VantaNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 ŌåÆ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED)        ŌåÉ after the single re-mint attempt
403 ŌåÆ ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 ŌåÆ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)            ŌĆö logged as warning
5xx ŌåÆ ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   CONNECTED)
other ŌåÆ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `client_id` or `client_secret` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. Vanta shows the secret only once at OAuth-app creation ŌĆö rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at install | Token endpoint returned 401 ŌĆö wrong client_id / client_secret | Verify the credential pair in the Vanta OAuth Apps screen. Rotate if uncertain. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, PENDING)` at install | Token endpoint unreachable (DNS / TLS / outbound firewall) | Verify the host running the connector can reach `https://api.vanta.com`. Whitelist the egress IP if your network requires it. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 ŌĆö OAuth app's scopes don't include the API surface you called | In Vanta ŌåÆ Settings ŌåÆ API ŌåÆ OAuth Apps, edit the app and add the required scope (`vanta-api.all:read` covers the read surfaces). |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 ŌĆö token expired AND the forced re-mint also failed | Usually the OAuth client was rotated or revoked. Re-issue `client_id` + `client_secret` in Vanta and re-install. |
| `vanta.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Vanta per-tenant rate cap) | The client retries with `Retry-After` then exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests in the sync. |
| 404 on `get_framework` / `get_control` etc. | Wrong UUID, or the resource was deleted in Vanta | Re-list the parent collection (`list_frameworks`) to find the current UUID. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'VantaNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed ŌĆö `VantaNetworkError = VantaServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
